meaning: ly//adv.鏈夊尯鍒湴adj.鏈夊垽鍒姏
examples: It is not discriminative to evaluate either a child's physical condition or his intellectual level.
This is not to say that the procedure employed in studies of discriminative learning will be quite without effect.
The discriminative purchase policy in public purchase is of anti-competition by nature. It stops the resources from free flow therefore we should be against it.
We must understand economics well enough to move beyond populist sentiment and support less discriminative tax policies.
So-called discriminative analysis confirms and deepens an appreciation of his artistry.
